title: Figure 3.8  The λ plane, the γ plane, and their mapping.
summary: This page explains how locations in the continuous-time λ plane map to locations in the discrete-time γ plane through γ = e^λ. It shows that the imaginary axis maps to the unit circle, the left half-plane maps inside the unit circle, and the right half-plane maps outside, corresponding respectively to constant-amplitude oscillation, exponential decay, and exponential growth of γ^n.
